preamble: The shift from the full vowel [æ] to the mid-central vowel [ə] (schwa) is a classic example of vowel reduction. In stress-timed languages like English, vowels in unstressed syllables lose their peripheral qualities and centralize.

examples.note: When stress shifts away from the first syllable, the [æ] reduces to a schwa.
Reduction of the vowel in unstressed compound endings.

context: In unstressed syllables in Catalan; conditioned by stress placement
allophones_note: Catalan vowel reduction in unstressed position
